title: Your Job Is Not Your Entire Identity
subtitle: 你的工作不等于你：如何摆脱职业身份危机

paragraphs.en: When someone asks us "what do you do?" we nearly always reply with our occupation. Work, for many of us, is much more than a job. It is the defining aspect of our identity. For many of us it is through our job that we can define ourselves.
paragraphs.zh: 当有人问我们“你是做什么的？”时，我们几乎总是以职业作答。对许多人而言，工作远不只是一份差事，它是我们身份的决定性方面。对很多人来说，我们正是通过工作来定义自己的。

words.meaning: adj. 决定性的；最典型的

paragraphs.en: "Without my job I don't know who I am," is a sentence that has been uttered on more than a handful of occasions from my office chair. Indeed, it can be one of the most challenging aspects I work on with clients who have lost or been forced into changing their jobs. This loss provokes an identity crisis much greater than the loss of the job itself.
paragraphs.zh: “没有工作，我就不知道自己是谁”——这句话从我办公室的椅子上已听过不止一次。事实上，这正是我与那些失业或被迫换工作的来访者合作时，所面临的最具挑战性的方面之一。这种丧失引发的身份危机，远比失去工作本身要严重得多。

paragraphs.en: One of the things I have come to understand, however, is that our identity is much more complex than we recognise at first glance. If we take the time to reflect we might recognise that as well as our work we can also identify as a friend, a spouse, a parent, or a member of a community.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，我逐渐认识到的一点是，我们的身份远比乍看之下要复杂得多。如果我们花时间反思，可能会意识到，除了工作身份外，我们还可以认同自己是朋友、配偶、父母，或某个社群的一员。

paragraphs.en: We may recognise that we feel and act differently in these roles than we do at work. The passive daughter becomes an assertive leader at work. Furthermore, our identities at work are not static. They change over time.
paragraphs.zh: 我们或许会发现，自己在这些角色中的感受和行为，与在工作时截然不同。那个在家中温顺被动的女儿，在工作上可能变成一位果敢自信的领导者。此外，我们的职业身份也并非一成不变，它们会随时间推移而改变。

paragraphs.en: I myself have been a shop assistant, a waitress, a student, and a clinical psychologist. At each stage my ability to adapt to and develop my career identity has been crucial to my wellbeing. Whilst we like to eliminate uncertainty, we have to manage it, especially in today's volatile job market.
paragraphs.zh: 我自己就曾当过店员、女服务员、学生，如今是一名临床心理学家。在每个阶段，我适应和发展职业身份的能力都对我的身心健康至关重要。尽管我们总想消除不确定性，但我们必须学会应对它，尤其是在当今动荡多变的就业市场中。

paragraphs.en: How we see ourselves is central to our identity. When we tell ourselves "I'm good at starting projects but not so great at seeing them through" it can become part of our belief system. But if you have the unfortunate experience of an enforced job change, you will need to examine those beliefs to see how grounded in reality they are.
paragraphs.zh: 我们如何看待自己，是身份问题的核心。当我们告诉自己“我擅长启动项目，但不太擅长贯彻到底”时，这就会成为我们信念体系的一部分。但如果你不幸遭遇了被迫换工作的情况，你就需要审视这些信念，看看它们在现实中究竟有多少根据。

paragraphs.en: We can change our beliefs, behaviours and emotional experience at any time through experimentation, practice and conscious self-discipline. In an age where career progression may lead us into new sectors, it is ever more important to challenge our sense of self. You can create a new experience of your identity by changing the beliefs you hold about yourself.
paragraphs.zh: 我们随时可以通过尝试、实践和有意识的自律，来改变自己的信念、行为和情感体验。在一个职业发展可能引领我们进入全新领域的时代，挑战自我认知变得愈发重要。你可以通过改变对自己的信念，来创造一种全新的身份体验。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately it is you who define who you are. You are only your job if you let it be so.
paragraphs.zh: 归根结底，是你自己决定了你是谁。只有在你允许的情况下，你的工作才等同于你。
